items.title: [负面] [h1] The most thoroughly dissected review of this ...
items.summary:
[h1] The most thoroughly dissected review of this game on Steam. [/h1]

In this review I might come to address issues by comparing them to other Gachas like WW or GI.

[h3]Pulls[/h3]

Let’s start off by the ‘generosity’ that is being suggested, while yes there is an initial large amount of pulls and free [b]Awaker[/b] the [b]Enlighten[/b] that get unlocked (similar to constellations in GI) are widely different in power.
They also have 15 tiers of Passive Skills which very quickly scales away, and I’ll get into how this affects the game's real gacha mechanic in the gameplay section.

From my initial pulls I managed to get 4x [b]Enlighten[/b] with Alva (I don’t know if that was luck or scripted) the reason I am hesitant to say this was just a “fluke” is because the game immediately attempted to sell me more [b]Enlighten[/b] tiers when that happened. 

So let's talk about the [b]Awakening[/b] rules/rates, you only need 30 pulls to get a certain SSR which could be considered ‘generous’, however looking at the pity in the Character Limited banner (the one that usually matters in Gachas) there is only a 33.33% to pull the character initially, followed by 66.66% and then finally 100%. Making it similar or rather equal to the competition which is CRAZY considering the amount of [b]Enlighten[/b] you can build up on [b]Awakers[/b]. 

The question that also persists with these gachas is that while yes, they might seem initially generous, what does the availability of currency look like into the future? Personally I find it to look rather grim.

[h3]Gameplay[/h3]

If you like deckbuilders it is kinda cool I guess, there are currently four [b]Designated Realm[/b] types [b]Chaos[/b], [b]Caro[/b], [b]Aequor[/b] and [b]Ultra[/b] which allows there to be quite a lot of diversity in the decks.
All the [b]Designated Realm[/b] types have different mechanics built-in.

[b]Chaos[/b] can be played with ONE other [b]Designated Realm[/b] typing.

[b]Aequor[/b] summons Tentacles.

[b]Caro[/b] creates a [b]Embryo[/b] card which they can either consume for [b]Awaker[/b] [b][Aliemus Blowup] [/b](similar to Burst in GI) or [b]Devour[/b] through the [b]Caro [Aliemus Blowup][/b].

[b]Ultra[/b] playing cards can give you an extra turn.

These mechanics all get infinitely more difficult/involved with unique character effects, card effects or items.
[b]Ultra[/b] being advertised as the most difficult one is the one I’ve currently played the least simply because of the amount of unique card effects and partly because the translation is rather poor.
If I only had to judge the game on the mechanics of the cards it would be fantastic.

The gameplay loop is mind numbingly boring (OPINION), and there are a lot of details you could get into but the game is ultimately ‘free’ so if you are interested in testing the mechanics and gameplay do so.

What I did notice and what stood out to me was the [b]Death Resistance[/b] this is something similar to Darkest Dungeon. Where you are intentionally supposed to hover around death and your [b]Death Resistance[/b] can protect you from dying. Your items, characters and cards can all improve your [b]Death Resistance[/b].

This is the REAL gacha because by hovering the player around a death mechanic, rng and an increasing level of required stats they can essentially force players to pay more. There is also an item called [b]Emergency Cognitive Unit[/b] this item allows your characters to come back to full life, full [b]Aliemus[/b] after death allowing you to practically clear any obstacle you have. These bad boys can be purchased with in-game currency.

[h3]Opinion & Comparison[/h3]

Comparing this game to the other gachas is to make something bad look worse, this game is horribly bogged down in stats, acquisition and game mechanics. There is no real ‘free play’, your character is a png with sad effects and even sadder character models.

They even have the absolute gall to attempt to have the same pricing as GI or WW. 
It really is laughable.

No, this game isn’t generous.
No, this game isn’t fun.
No, this game adds nothing new to the genre.

Get a good deckbuilder game (Slay the Spire) and then play a different Gacha. This game fails at both.

items.title: [正面] 今年玩过的最好玩的二次元氪金手游 画风设定跟玩法都很对我胃口 美术和设定跟边狱巴士非常相似 不过两者...
items.summary: 今年玩过的最好玩的二次元氪金手游 画风设定跟玩法都很对我胃口 美术和设定跟边狱巴士非常相似 不过两者都是克系就还好 至于玩法上有相当多杀戮尖塔的影子 甚至我第四喜欢的故障机器人都还原了 转生到深海之后还是我第四喜欢的派系 太对了 大伙想玩建议先搞个港服 据说过段时间就能互通了
